text.title: User Story Mapping Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every story-map enforces.

rule.statement: R1 (walking skeleton) MUST contain at least one story per activity. A release without coverage across every activity is not a walking skeleton.
rule.rationale: R1 must traverse the user journey end-to-end; partial R1s ship dead-end products.

rule.statement: Every story carries persona_id linking to the persona inventory. Untagged stories or "all users" tags are rejected.
rule.rationale: Persona-less stories mask conflicting needs and produce kitchen-sink scope.

rule.statement: Map lives as YAML in git as single source of truth. Markdown / Mermaid / Miro views are emitted from the YAML, not edited independently.
rule.rationale: Multi-source maps diverge; YAML-in-git keeps a single canonical structure.

rule.statement: Map declares ≤8 top-level activities. More than 8 means the scope is too broad; split by persona or product line.

rationale.source: Miller, magical number seven, plus or minus two (1956)

rule.rationale: More than 8 activities exceeds the audience's working memory at workshop time.

rule.statement: Vertical bands are fixed: R1 (walking skeleton), R2 (minimum lovable), R3 (delight), R4 (nice-to-have). No custom band names; no inserted bands.
rule.rationale: Custom bands defeat cross-map comparison and roadmap negotiation.

example.title: Map fragment satisfying r1+r2
example.code: activities: - id: act-discover name: Discover catalogue tasks: - id: task-browse stories: - {id: s-1, title: "Browse top categories", persona_id: shopper, release: R1} - {id: s-2, title: "Filter by brand", persona_id: shopper, release: R2} - id: act-purchase name: Purchase tasks: - id: task-checkout stories: - {id: s-3, title: "Pay with card", persona_id: shopper, release: R1}

example.title: Map fragment failing r2
example.code: activities: - id: act-1 tasks: - stories: - {id: s-1, title: "Filter products"}
